CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT to repeal an act entitled "An act to regulate and provide
for the advertisement of sales of real estate under judgments
of courts of equity and executions in Morgan county ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the act,entitled"An act to regulate and pro-
vide for the advertisement of sales of real estate under
judgments of courts of equity and executions in Morgan
county," which was approved March twenty -sixth, one
thousand eight hundred and eighty-six,be,and the same
is hereby , repealed.
Approved February 14 ,1896.

CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT to amend sectionone thousand two hundred and eighty-
four of chapter thirty-six, article four, title"Crimes and Pun-
ishments ," of the Kentucky Statutes , relating to prize fighting.
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That all of section one thousand two hundred and
eighty-four of the Kentucky Statutes, chapter thirty-six,
articlefour, subdivisionfive, title"Crimes and Punish-
ments ," beginning immediately afterthe word "called" in
K.S., c. 36,the thirdlinethereof,be and the same ishereby repealed,p . 523 .

and in lieuthereof the following is hereby enacted, viz.:
"He shall upon convictionbe deemed guilty of a felony
and confined in the penitentiaryfor a period of not less
than one,nor more than fiveyears," so that said section
when thus amended shall read as follows :
"§ 1284. If any person shallengage in a prize fight, or
a fightfor a bet, wager or stakes, by whatever name it
may be called, he shall, upon conviction, be deemed
guiltyof a felony,and confined in the penitentiaryfor a
period of not less than one nor more than fiveyears ."
§ 2. This act shalltake effectupon itsapproval by the
Governor and at the expirationof ninety days next after
the finaladjournment of this session of the General As-
sembly .
Approved March 14 , 1896 .

CHAPTER 7 .
AN ACT to create in this Commonwealth a more speedy remedy
for the setting aside of fraudulent conveyances .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That hereafter in this Commonwealth it shall be
lawful for any party who may be aggrieved thereby,
when any real property has been fraudulently conveyed ,
transferredor mortgaged ,to file,in a court having juris-
dictionof the subject matter ,a petitionin equity against
the partiesto such fraudulent transfer or conveyance or
mortgage , or their representatives or heirs, alleging
thereinthe factsshowing theirright of action and alleg-
ing such fraud, or the facts constitutingit, and describ-
ing such property and when done a lispendens shall be
created upon the property so described, and said suit
shall progress and be determined as other suits in equity ,

CHAPTER 8 .
AN ACT to repeal section one of an act approved February ninth ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty , entitled An act to
amend an act to maintain a graded school in Hartford ," and
acts amendatory thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section one of an act, entitled"An act to
amend an act to maintain a graded school in Hartford ,"
and the acts amendatory thereof, approved February
ninth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty, be, and
the same is hereby, repealed.
§ 2. That so much of section three of an act, entitled
" An act in relation to the Hartford graded school,
known as Hartford College," approved April twenty-
sixth, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-six, as au-
thorize or attempt to authorize the levy and collection
of a tax to maintain said school be,and the same ishere-
by , repealed. This act shallnot affector render invalid
any tax leviedpriorto January first, one thousand eight
hundred and ninety-six.
§ 3. Owing to the facts that there are grave doubts ex-
istingas to the legalityof the taxesleviedto maintain
the Hartford graded school, commonly known as Hart-
ford College, and to avoid confusion should said tax be
declared illegal, an emergency is hereby declared, and
this act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspassage .
Approved March 16 , 1896.

wealth of Kentucky :

by a majority of theirboard ,the county court consenting,
are hereby authorized and empowered to sell, transfer
and convey by deed, the titlein and to said seminary

what is ordinary, as shall be equitable, and make good

in and to said property to the trustees of said common

amauga ,and made a National Park of same ;and , where-
